digest: fctry is a Claude Code plugin that orchestrates eight specialized agents to convert conversational user visions into complete NLSpec v2 specifications and then drive autonomous builds from them. The system enforces an experience-first contract where the spec captures what the user sees, does, and feels, and every user-facing surface speaks plain English through a single translation module, blocking factory-internal vocabulary. The user approves a build plan once, after which the system executes all chunks autonomously, handling failures and rearchitecting silently while resurfacing only for experience-level questions. The live spec viewer provides a multi-project dashboard with a recursive kanban board, real-time updates via WebSocket, and mission control during builds showing a visual dependency graph and activity feed. The system uses Node.js, SQLite, WebSocket, Mermaid.js, and the Claude Code Plugin API. Unbuilt features include background-worker execution for parallel chunk processing, a viewer-as-control-plane with a terminal emulator and MCP elicitation, cross-project synthesis, a self-improvement loop via `/fctry:reflect`, an arbiter-tier disagreement resolver for build-time verification, incremental build trace parsing, and an experimental fully-autonomous loop that runs the discover-incorporate-build-verify cycle without human direction.
